message:
fix(search,analytics): harden hit dedup hashing, add SQL injection guards, and warn on missing Tantivy index

Three categories of improvements across the search and analytics subsystems:

1. SQL injection prevention in analytics queries (src/analytics/query.rs):
   - `table_exists()` and `table_has_column()` both interpolate caller-supplied
     names directly into PRAGMA statements via format!(), which is a classic
     injection vector. Added alphanumeric+underscore validation on the table
     and column name parameters, returning false early for any input containing
     unexpected characters. While these functions are only called with internal
     constants today, the guard makes the API safe for future callers.
   - Added a clarifying comment on the mm_coverage_pct calculation to explain
     why dividing message_metrics.row_count by total_messages is correct (PK
     on message_id guarantees unique coverage count).

2. Stable hit hash collision fix (src/search/query.rs):
   - The previous `stable_hit_hash()` had a subtle bug: when content was
     non-empty, it returned `stable_content_hash(content)` immediately via
     an early return, discarding the source_path, line_number, and created_at
     components entirely. This meant two different search hits with identical
     content but different file paths or line numbers would hash to the same
     value, causing cross-file deduplication collisions.
   - Restructured the function so the content hash (when present) is used as
     the initial seed instead of FNV_OFFSET, and then ALL subsequent fields
     are always folded in. A separator byte "|" is also prepended before
     source_path to prevent field-boundary ambiguity (e.g., content "abc"
     + path "def" vs content "abcdef" + path "").

3. Missing Tantivy index warnings (src/search/query.rs, src/lib.rs):
   - Added `has_tantivy()` accessor on SearchClient so callers can check
     whether the full-text index is available without reaching into internals.
   - In `SearchClient::open_with_options()`, added a tracing::warn when
     Tantivy is unavailable but SQLite exists — this situation means the
     client will silently fall back to SQL-only search, which is dramatically
     slower and returns worse results. The warning tells operators exactly
     how to fix it (`cass index --full`).
   - In `run_cli_search()` (lib.rs), added an eprintln warning for the same
     condition so interactive CLI users see the degradation immediately rather
     than wondering why results are poor.

4. Test update (src/search/tantivy.rs):
   - Bumped the expected index directory version from v6 to v7 in the
     `index_dir_creates_versioned_path` test to match the current schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
